stem: The area of a triangle is equal to x² square centimeters. The length of the base of the triangle is 2x + 30 centimeters, and the height of the triangle is x − 12 centimeters. What is the value of x?
rationale: The area is (1/2)(base)(height) = (1/2)(2x + 30)(x − 12) = (x + 15)(x − 12) = x² + 3x − 180. Setting this equal to x² gives 3x − 180 = 0, so x = 60.
